# omada_device_ap (Resource)

Manages the configuration of an Omada AP device. APs cannot be created or deleted via the API — this resource manages the configuration of an already-adopted AP. Import by MAC address. Delete removes from Terraform state only.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# APs cannot be created or deleted via the API. This resource manages
# the configuration of an already-adopted AP. Destroying the resource
# removes it from Terraform state only.
resource "omada_device_ap" "example" {
  mac           = "9C-A2-F4-00-08-12"
  name          = "Office-AP"
  wlan_group_id = omada_wlan_group.default.id

  # Radio settings
  radio_2g_enable         = true
  radio_2g_channel_width  = "20"
  radio_2g_channel        = "0" # Auto
  radio_2g_tx_power_level = 4

  radio_5g_enable         = true
  radio_5g_channel_width  = "80"
  radio_5g_channel        = "0" # Auto
  radio_5g_tx_power_level = 4

  # General settings
  led_setting     = 2 # Site settings
  ip_setting_mode = "dhcp"
  lldp_enable     = 1

  # Management VLAN
  management_vlan_enable     = true
  management_vlan_network_id = omada_network.management.id

  # OFDMA
  ofdma_enable_2g = true
  ofdma_enable_5g = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `mac` (String) The AP MAC address (unique identifier). Used for import.

### Optional

- `ip_setting_mode` (String) IP address mode: 'dhcp' or 'static'.
- `l3_access_enable` (Boolean) Enable L3 management access.
- `lb_2g_enable` (Boolean) Enable load balancing on 2.4GHz.
- `lb_2g_max_clients` (Number) Max clients for 2.4GHz load balancing.
- `lb_5g_enable` (Boolean) Enable load balancing on 5GHz.
- `lb_5g_max_clients` (Number) Max clients for 5GHz load balancing.
- `led_setting` (Number) LED setting: 0=Off, 1=On, 2=Follow site setting.
- `lldp_enable` (Number) LLDP: 0=Off, 1=On, 2=Follow site setting. Null on APs that don't support LLDP.
- `loopback_detect_enable` (Boolean) Enable loopback detection.
- `management_vlan_enable` (Boolean) Enable management VLAN.
- `management_vlan_network_id` (String) The LAN network ID for the management VLAN.
- `name` (String) The display name of the AP.
- `ofdma_enable_2g` (Boolean) Enable OFDMA on 2.4GHz.
- `ofdma_enable_5g` (Boolean) Enable OFDMA on 5GHz.
- `radio_2g_channel` (String) 2.4GHz channel (e.g., '0' for auto, '1'-'11' for specific).
- `radio_2g_channel_width` (String) 2.4GHz channel width (e.g., '0' for auto, '1' for 20MHz, '2' for 40MHz).
- `radio_2g_enable` (Boolean) Enable 2.4GHz radio.
- `radio_2g_tx_power` (Number) 2.4GHz TX power in dBm (used when tx_power_level=3/Custom).
- `radio_2g_tx_power_level` (Number) 2.4GHz TX power level: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Custom, 4=Auto.
- `radio_5g_channel` (String) 5GHz channel (e.g., '0' for auto, specific channel numbers).
- `radio_5g_channel_width` (String) 5GHz channel width (e.g., '0' for auto, '2' for 40MHz, '4' for 80MHz).
- `radio_5g_enable` (Boolean) Enable 5GHz radio. Null on 2.4GHz-only APs.
- `radio_5g_tx_power` (Number) 5GHz TX power in dBm (used when tx_power_level=3/Custom).
- `radio_5g_tx_power_level` (Number) 5GHz TX power level: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Custom, 4=Auto.
- `rssi_2g_enable` (Boolean) Enable RSSI threshold on 2.4GHz.
- `rssi_2g_threshold` (Number) RSSI threshold for 2.4GHz (negative dBm).
- `rssi_5g_enable` (Boolean) Enable RSSI threshold on 5GHz.
- `rssi_5g_threshold` (Number) RSSI threshold for 5GHz (negative dBm).
- `wlan_group_id` (String) The WLAN group ID assigned to this AP.

### Read-Only

- `firmware_version` (String) The AP firmware version. Read-only.
- `ip` (String) The AP IP address. Read-only.
- `model` (String) The AP model (e.g., 'EAP655-Wall'). Read-only.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by MAC address
terraform import omada_device_ap.example 9C-A2-F4-00-08-12
```
